en.extract:
The Eastern yellow-billed hornbill (Tockus flavirostris), also known as the northern yellow-billed hornbill, is a species of hornbill in the family Bucerotidae. It is found in Djibouti, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Kenya, Somalia, South Sudan, Tanzania, and Uganda. It resembles the southern yellow-billed hornbill, but has blackish (not pinkish) skin around the eyes.

The '''Eastern yellow-billed hornbill''' ('''''Tockus flavirostris'''''), also known as the '''northern yellow-billed hornbill''',<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" /> is a species of [[hornbill]] in the family [[Bucerotidae]]. It is found in [[Djibouti]], [[Eritrea]], [[Ethiopia]], [[Kenya]], [[Somalia]], [[South Sudan]], [[Tanzania]], and [[Uganda]]. It resembles the [[southern yellow-billed hornbill]], but has blackish (not pinkish) skin around the eyes.
